---
title: "MIRAGE Project"
site: workflowr::wflow_site
output: workflowr::wflow_html
---

## Project Overview

This workflowr website organizes technical notes for the MIRAGE project: a Bayesian framework for gene-level rare-variant association analysis using variant-level summary counts and functional annotations.

The goal of the site is to keep the project reproducible as it develops. It records the statistical model, implementation questions, toy examples, runtime-improvement plans, and a lightweight project log. The site is built from R Markdown source files in `analysis/` and rendered into `docs/` for GitHub Pages.

## What MIRAGE Does

MIRAGE, short for mixture-model-based rare-variant analysis on genes, is designed for settings where rare-variant counts are available from ancestry-matched case-control studies or transmitted/untransmitted family data. At a high level, MIRAGE treats variants in a gene as a mixture of risk and non-risk variants rather than assuming every included rare variant contributes equally.

Functional annotation categories, such as loss-of-function or predicted damaging missense groups, are used to define prior probabilities that a variant is a risk variant. These probabilities are estimated across genes with an empirical Bayes strategy. The resulting output includes gene-level Bayes factors, posterior probabilities, and Bayesian FDR summaries.

The language on this website is intentionally cautious: MIRAGE can improve power in settings where sparse, heterogeneous rare-variant effects are plausible, but its validity still depends on study design, input quality, annotation choices, and careful control of population structure.

## Website Contents

- [Method](method.html): notation, generative model, conditional likelihood, EM algorithm, gene-level Bayes factor, posterior probability, Bayesian FDR, and model limitations.
- [Speed](speed.html): runtime bottlenecks, optimization priorities, profiling plan, caching strategy, log-scale computation, vectorized aggregation, and parallelization.
- [Examples](examples.html): fake toy input table, optional local MIRAGE run, expected output objects, and interpretation notes.
- [Notes](notes.html): project log, remaining implementation questions, profiling next steps, data-access-dependent example planning, and reproducibility checklist.

## Data Privacy Statement

This repository and website must not include real WES, ASD, dbGaP, SPARK, protected, private, or large data files. The example page uses toy data only. Real summary statistics, individual-level data, protected annotations, and large intermediate outputs should remain outside the GitHub repository unless data access, consent, and sharing rules explicitly allow public release.
